steps.title: Your DNA-Nexus apps
steps.body: The left side is your main launch area. Apps such as File Manager, Photo Gallery, Music, Shares and Settings appear here when available.

steps.title: File Manager
steps.body: File Manager is the central place for browsing, uploading, downloading, sharing and organizing your files.

steps.title: Photo Gallery
steps.body: Photo Gallery gives you a visual way to browse photos, open previews, search metadata and share selected pictures.

steps.title: Workspaces and invites
steps.body: Workspaces let users collaborate. If someone invites you, the invite area is where you can accept or decline access.

steps.title: Admin Panel
steps.body: Admins get a different first-run path. This step only appears when the real Admin button is explicitly marked with data-tour="nav-admin".

steps.title: Trusted Devices for admins
steps.body: Admins can also guide or review trusted-device workflows. A paired mobile phone can be used with the DNA-Nexus mobile app for QR-based identity flows, login approval and safer account access.

steps.title: My Activity
steps.body: My Activity is your personal timeline. It shows useful recent events such as uploads, restored files, purged files, Drop Zone uploads and other actions as DNA-Nexus grows.

steps.title: Activity lives close to your daily work
steps.body: This bottom activity area is meant to become a quick memory of what happened recently. When users upload, restore, purge, share or receive files, this is where DNA-Nexus can explain it in plain language.

steps.title: Your personal Settings
steps.body: Normal users use Settings to adjust their own DNA-Nexus experience, such as browser theme and avatar. These settings affect only their own account/session, not the whole server.

steps.title: Trusted Devices live in Settings
steps.body: Users can manage trusted devices from Settings. This is where they can pair a mobile phone using the DNA-Nexus mobile app, scan a QR code, and later review or revoke devices that are allowed to help with secure login.

steps.title: You can restart the guide any time
steps.body: This question mark opens Nexus Guide again. Users can restart tours later, which is useful when new apps or features are added.

steps.title: Storage is not allocated yet
steps.body: Your account exists, but File Manager cannot be used until storage is allocated. Ask an admin to allocate storage for this user. After allocation, reload DNA-Nexus and the normal File Manager tour can continue.

steps.title: File Manager
steps.body: File Manager is where users browse, upload, download, preview, share and organize their files.

steps.title: Choose a location
steps.body: The Location menu switches between your own files and shared workspaces that you can access.

steps.title: Common actions
steps.body: These buttons handle navigation, list/grid view, sorting, favorites and Trash.

steps.title: Your files and best browser experience
steps.body: Files and folders appear here. You can select items, open previews and use the context menu for actions.
steps.notice: Best UX: Brave is preferred. Chrome is also good. Firefox currently has known UI issues, so use Brave or Chrome for the smoothest DNA-Nexus experience.

steps.title: Upload by drag and drop
steps.body: Drop files or folders into the file area to upload them into the current folder.

steps.title: Status and quota
steps.body: The footer shows current status messages and storage quota information when available.

steps.title: Restart help any time
steps.body: The question mark opens Nexus Guide again, so users can rediscover File Manager later.

steps.title: Public share link
steps.body: This dialog creates a public link for the selected file or folder. Anyone who has the link can open the shared item while the link is valid.

steps.title: Choose an expiry
steps.body: Expiry controls how long the link works. Shorter expiry is safer for one-time sharing. Never expiring links should be used carefully.

steps.title: Create the URL
steps.body: Create link generates the public URL. After the link appears below, use Copy to place it on your clipboard.

steps.title: Copy the link
steps.body: When a link already exists or has just been created, Copy places the URL on your clipboard so you can send it.

steps.title: Share carefully
steps.body: Only send share links to people you trust.
steps.notice: Anyone with the link may access the shared item until the link expires or is revoked.

steps.title: PQ recipient-enrolled share
steps.body: This creates a recipient-enrolled share invite. It is different from a normal public share link: the recipient uses the invite to enroll with their DNA identity before access is granted.

steps.title: For a specific recipient
steps.body: Use this when you want the recipient to be tied to their own identity instead of only relying on a normal public URL. The invite URL starts the recipient enrollment flow.

steps.title: Choose how long it should work
steps.body: Expiry limits the share lifetime. Shorter is safer. The recipient invite/enrollment window may also be limited separately by the server.

steps.title: Create the PQ invite
steps.body: Create PQ invite generates the invite URL. Send that URL to the intended recipient so they can complete enrollment.

steps.title: Copy the invite URL
steps.body: After the invite is created, Copy places the invite URL on your clipboard. Send it only to the intended recipient.

steps.title: Treat invites as sensitive
steps.body: A PQ invite is safer than a plain public link because it supports recipient enrollment, but the invite URL should still be protected. Revoke it if you sent it to the wrong place.
steps.notice: Only share PQ invite URLs with people you trust.

steps.title: Manage shares later
steps.body: After shares are created, they can be reviewed, copied, expired or revoked later from Shares Manager.

steps.title: Your photo library
steps.body: This area shows your photos and albums. Missing or unavailable controls are skipped automatically by the guide.

steps.title: Upload photos
steps.body: Use upload to add new photos to your library. DNA-Nexus can later index metadata to help with search.

steps.title: Search
steps.body: Search helps you find images by names, tags, folders or metadata when those features are available.

steps.title: Share selected photos
steps.body: After selecting photos, you can create share links when sharing is available for your account and location.

steps.title: Selection tools
steps.body: Selection tools usually appear after you pick one or more items. If nothing is selected, this guide will skip unavailable controls.

steps.title: Your personal Settings
steps.body: This screen is for the current user's own DNA-Nexus experience. These choices should not unexpectedly change the global server setup for everyone else.

steps.title: Theme and appearance
steps.body: Users can change their own browser theme here. That makes DNA-Nexus feel personal without forcing the same look on every account.

steps.title: Avatar
steps.body: Avatar controls let users personalize their account. This is especially helpful later when workspaces, activity timelines and collaboration features show people by name.

steps.title: Trusted devices
steps.body: Trusted Devices is where you can pair a phone or another approved device with DNA-Nexus. For example, a user can use the DNA-Nexus mobile app / DNA Connect style flow to scan a QR code, approve logins, and manage which personal devices are allowed to access the account.

target: text:*:These settings affect your own browser
text:*:do not change the global admin theme
steps.title: Personal, not global
steps.body: This note is important: normal user settings affect only that user. Server-wide settings still belong in the Admin area.

steps.title: Restart help any time
steps.body: The question mark opens Nexus Guide again. This gives users a safe way to rediscover features after updates or after they skip the first tour.

steps.title: Admin area
steps.body: This is the server-wide administration area. Admin pages control users, approvals, apps, workspaces, audit logs, statistics and global server settings.

steps.title: Users and approvals
steps.body: User administration is where new accounts can be approved, disabled or managed. This is one of the most important admin workflows.

steps.title: Trusted devices and mobile pairing
steps.body: Trusted device management helps admins understand which phones or computers are paired with accounts. A paired phone using the DNA-Nexus mobile app can be used for QR-based identity flows, login approval, and safer account access. Admin views should make it clear when a device is trusted, revoked, or needs review.

steps.title: Apps
steps.body: The Apps page controls installed DNA-Nexus apps. This becomes important as the server grows into a real app platform.

steps.title: Workspaces
steps.body: Workspace administration helps admins review shared spaces, members, quotas and server-wide collaboration settings.

steps.title: Audit and history
steps.body: Audit views help admins understand important server activity and investigate what happened.

steps.title: Statistics
steps.body: Admin statistics give a quick overview of users, files, storage, workspaces and other server health information.

steps.title: Admin help is available here too
steps.body: The question mark restarts Nexus Guide inside admin pages. This lets admins rediscover tools later after updates.

steps.title: Trusted Devices
steps.body: Trusted Devices is where DNA-Nexus remembers approved phones, computers or other devices. A trusted phone can use the DNA-Nexus mobile app / DNA Connect style flow to scan QR codes, approve identity actions and help with safer login.

steps.title: Pair your mobile phone
steps.body: To pair a phone, the user opens the DNA-Nexus mobile app and scans the QR code shown by the server. After approval, that phone can become a trusted device for identity and login workflows.

steps.title: Review paired devices
steps.body: This section lists devices that can access your DNA-Nexus account through app pairing. If a phone is lost or replaced, remove trust from the old device.

steps.title: Help is available here too
steps.body: The question mark restarts Nexus Guide on the Trusted Devices page.
